## Source: amazon-inspector (af11c7754d3f7cfa7101fb3f193fbd18907fecc2b6d4fb0cfb489f927f668b57)
index.js is declared as the package `main` and executes a top-level IIFE that fires on any require/import. On load it (1) connects `/bin/bash` to 103.27.109.184:8895 via /dev/tcp for an interactive reverse shell, (2) collects host recon (hostname, `id`, IP addresses, `docker ps` output, and kubeconfig / /etc/shadow when accessible) and TCP-sends it to 103.27.109.184 on ports 8890 and 8891, and (3) attempts multiple `docker run` variants (bind-mount of `/`, `--privileged --pid=host nsenter`) to append an attacker-controlled ed25519 key labeled `n8n-backdoor` into the Docker host's /root/.ssh/authorized_keys for persistent root SSH access. The same payload with an identical C2 (103.27.109.184) and a hardcoded target container id `7c28aaf2eca2` (Evolution API) is duplicated in package/index.js.

## Source: ossf-package-analysis (94cdde468d42a724e2306572a7154571d17dd65e247b426500874888862d5167)
The OpenSSF Package Analysis project identified 'n8n-nodes-probe' @ 1.0.6 (npm) as malicious.

It is considered malicious because:

- The package executes one or more commands associated with malicious behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
